guidance.promptSnippet: Track only genuinely complex multi-step work.
promptGuidelines: Use todo only when the current request has 3 or more meaningful, non-trivial steps, explicit dependencies, multiple user-requested items, or a real risk of losing track during a long task.
Do not create todos for single tasks, simple edits, short searches, one-command checks, purely informational answers, or exploratory investigation before concrete follow-up work is known.
Do not create a one-item todo list. If the work cannot be broken into multiple useful, verifiable steps, just do the work directly.
Avoid filler plans such as generic investigate/implement/test lists. Each todo must describe a specific, actionable outcome that helps track real progress.
Keep exactly one item in_progress while working. Mark items completed as soon as they are fully done, and never mark incomplete or blocked work as completed.
When all requested work is complete, mark remaining items completed and clear the todo list if the tool supports clearing, so no completed-only todo UI remains visible.
